2026-04-15T20:54:06.0764397Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-15T20:54:06.0764845Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-15T20:54:06.0765212Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-15T20:54:06.0765520Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-15T20:54:06.0765754Z [36;1m[0m

It also added `sdk/core/Azure.Core/tests/samples/CredentialsSamples.cs` and updated `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` with a callout note that links to `MigrationGuide.md`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `Azure.Core` README\\u0026#39;s new **Authenticating Service Clients** section only shows the happy-path code snippet but provides no pointer to the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) for users who encounter `CS0433` compile errors or runtime `TypeLoadException` when mixing package versions.\\n- `Azure.Identity` README and CHANGELOG correctly link to the Migration Guide; `Azure.Core` README does not — leaving users who arrive via `Azure.Core` documentation without any troubleshooting path.\\n- The section could also benefit from a brief note that the `Azure.Identity` namespace is still used (types are forwarded), so existing `using Azure.Identity;` directives remain valid.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`\\n\\nLocate the **Authenticating Service Clients** section (around line 238). After the closing code fence of the `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` snippet, add the following paragraph:\\n\\n```markdown\\nIf you experience `CS0433` compile errors or `TypeLoadException` at runtime after upgrading to `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, see the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) for remediation steps. All credential types remain in the `Azure.Identity` namespace, so existing `using Azure.Identity;` directives are still valid.\\n```\\n\\nThe full section should look like:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticating Service Clients\\n\\nStarting with Azure.Core 1.53.0, all credential types previously in `Azure.Identity` are available directly from the Azure service client libraries, which all take a dependency on `Azure.Core`:\\n\\n```\\n// No Azure.Identity package reference required for client libraries that depend on Azure.Core 1.53.0+\\nvar credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\nvar client = new SecretClient(new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential);\\n```\\n\\nIf you experience `CS0433` compile errors or `TypeLoadException` at runtime after upgrading to `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, see the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) for remediation steps. All credential types remain in the `Azure.Identity` namespace, so existing `using Azure.Identity;` directives are still valid.\\n```\\n\\nNo new sample code or snippet changes are required — the existing `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` snippet in `CredentialsSamples.cs` is sufficient.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo changes needed — the snippet `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` was already added by PR #58082.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` already has all required sections. Only the **Authenticating Service Clients** subsection needs the prose addition described above.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — confirm README snippet block is unchanged (no placeholder was modified)\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24476305808/agentic_workflow) · ● 666.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58183\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-15T20:51:15Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-15T20:51:15Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

close.\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwzMDU5MDgwMjU3\"}],\"assignee\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjkxMzQ0NQ==\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/subscriptions\"},\"comments\":5,\"closed_at\":\"2026-03-02T06:59:45Z\",\"created_at\":\"2026-02-27T06:57:43Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-04T07:12:29Z\",\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56583\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56583\",\"comments_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56583/comments\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56583/events\",\"labels_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56583/labels{/name}\",\"repository_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56583/reactions\"},\"assignees\":[{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjkxMzQ0NQ==\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/subscriptions\"}],\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM7uY01I\"},{\"id\":3732908692,\"number\":54541,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[FEATURE REQ] Unified execution contract for retry, timeout, and cancellation semantics across Azure .NET SDKs\",\"body\":\"In large-scale, **distributed .NET applications** that **use multiple Azure .NET SDKs** (for example Service Bus, Event Hubs, Storage, Cosmos DB, Key Vault,), retry behavior, timeout enforcement, and **CancellationToken semantics** are currently inconsistent and difficult to reason about across SDK boundaries.\\n\\nEach SDK exposes retry options and accepts a CancellationToken, but the combined behavior across async/await boundaries, background processing, transport retries, and SDK-internal retry policies is non-deterministic in practice. This makes it difficult to enforce strict latency budgets, bounded retries, and predictable failure behavior in real-world distributed systems.\\n\\nIn particular:\\n\\n- Retry policies may execute at multiple layers without a clear, unified contract for total retries or total elapsed time.\\n- Timeout settings appear to be interpreted differently across SDKs, with no clear guidance on per-attempt versus global timeout behavior.\\n- CancellationToken propagation is inconsistent, sometimes stopping user code but not in-flight retries or transport operations.\\n\\nIt would be valuable to introduce or document a **unified execution contract across Azure .NET SDKs** that clearly defines how retries, timeouts, and cancellation interact. This could include guidance or primitives for enforcing a global execution budget, deterministic cancellation behavior, and consistent retry semantics across SDKs.\\n\\n**1. Sample code snippet: global execution budget wrapper (SDK-agnostic)**\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Diagnostics;\\nusing System.Threading;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\npublic static class ExecutionBudget\\n{\\n    // A global time budget that you expect ALL nested SDK calls to respect.\\n    public static async Task\\u003cT\\u003e RunAsync\\u003cT\\u003e(\\n        Func\\u003cCancellationToken, Task\\u003cT\\u003e\\u003e operation,\\n        TimeSpan budget,\\n        CancellationToken callerToken = default)\\n    {\\n        using var budgetCts = new CancellationTokenSource(budget);\\n        using var linkedCts = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(callerToken, budgetCts.Token);\\n\\n        var sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();\\n        try\\n        {\\n            return await operation(linkedCts.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);\\n        }\\n        finally\\n        {\\n            sw.Stop();\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\\"ExecutionBudget elapsed: {sw.Elapsed.TotalMilliseconds:n0} ms (budget: {budget.TotalMilliseconds:n0} ms)\\\");\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**2. Sample code snippet: two SDK calls under one budget (illustrative)**\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus;\\nusing Azure.Storage.Blobs;\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\npublic class MultiSdkBudgetExample\\n{\\n    public async Task RunAsync(\\n        BlobClient blob,\\n        ServiceBusSender sender,\\n        CancellationToken token)\\n    {\\n        await ExecutionBudget.RunAsync(async ct =\\u003e\\n        {\\n            // Call 1: Storage (HTTP)\\n            await blob.GetPropertiesAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]            // Call 2: Service Bus (AMQP)\\n            var msg = new ServiceBusMessage(\\\"test\\\") { MessageId = Guid.NewGuid().ToString(\\\"N\\\") };\\n            await sender.SendMessageAsync(msg, ct);\\n\\n            return 0;\\n        }, budget: TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3), callerToken=[REDACTED]    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**3. Sample config snippet: showing “retry knobs differ” across SDKs**\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar sbOptions = new ServiceBusClientOptions\\n{\\n    RetryOptions =\\n    {\\n        Mode = ServiceBusRetryMode.Exponential,\\n        MaxRetries = 5,\\n        Delay = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(200),\\n        MaxDelay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5),\\n        TryTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10) // important semantic question: per-try or global?\\n    }\\n};\\n```\\n\\nThis is not a request for changes to a specific SDK, but a cross-cutting design consideration intended to improve predictability, reliability, and operability in distributed, event-driven, and latency-sensitive
